author: catwith1hat
content:
Frankly, Claude Opus is your best shot. I have somewhere on my todo to point Claude to the SAFE github repos.

Not open source, I know, but realistically everything else is a waste of your time if you want results. If you want to try something open, maybe Qwen 3.6 27B is something you could try. https://www.transilience.ai/blog/how-is-qwen3-6-for-automated-pentesting has some benchmarks for it in the security area. Not super impressive, but also not bad given that Qwen runs on a 3090.

author: sky.cactus
content: Opus definitely doesn't meet the requirements for "open source tools".  I'm familiar with all of the open source models, what I'm looking for is a good harness/orchestrator.  Kimi K2.7, GLM 5.2, and Minimax M3 are all quite competent models, but still benefit from a good harness/orchestrator specific to security auditing.

author: catwith1hat
content: https://swival.dev/ , an open source harness, added an /audit skill recently, if I recall correctly.

author: catwith1hat
content: <@301186049323958275>  Maybe that's a skill you can try https://github.com/sanbir/solidity-auditor-skills ? It's a bit hard to judge whether all this prompting makes a difference. It probably does but it's just so hard to quantify.

author: sky.cactus
content: Looks like that only works on closed source harnesses.  Also, I was hoping for a full self contained auditing tool, not a general purpose tool with a couple custom prompts tacked on.  Good chance that what I want doesn't exist yet.

author: catwith1hat
content: Are you sure that swival can't load those skills? There is nothing codex/claude specific in the solidity-auditor-skills repo that I linked.

author: sky.cactus
content: I am not sure, I am basing that on the list of "Supported AI Platforms" at the top of the readme.

author: catwith1hat
content: In the worst case, you can hook up Claude (the harness) to a model of your choice. Not 100% what you are asking for, but 100% local if you need it to be.

author: catwith1hat
content: I saw that too and was skeptical. Searching for `codex` gives no hit in the code base other than the README.md

author: sky.cactus
content: What I'm looking for: Trying to figure out if there is a fully open source tool that one can point at a set of Solidity contracts and it will do a pretty good security audit of them using open weight models under the hood (no tight coupling with closed source or closed weight stuff).

author: sky.cactus
content: My guess is the answer is no, because everyone seems to like building around Claude/ChatGPT.

author: catwith1hat
content: Yeah, that feels similar to the first mover advantage of Nvidia. Everyone tests their codebase with Nvidia first. Same here for Claude it seems.
